summary: Soundboard source with solid, clear audio (minor AUD bleed) captures a powerhouse ’79 show highlighted by an epic Shakedown Street and killer second‑set jams.
review: The recording is a soundboard capture that sounds clean for most of the concert, though a brief audience‑sourced segment mutes Jerry’s vocals on Wharf Rat. Musically the night is a classic late‑1979 performance – a tight first set leads into a monster Shakedown Street, soaring Passenger, Ramblin’ Rose and an unforgettable Eyes of the World → Truckin' sequence that Deadheads repeatedly cite as a highlight.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 4‑5 stars, praising both the musical flow and the soundboard quality; only minor complaints about a muffled vocal segment keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced SBD for most of the set. A short AUD‑derived portion (post‑Wharf Rat) is a bit muffled and some reviewers note slight pitch/speed issues that benefit from minor correction.

standout_songs: China Cat > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds energy with tight, upbeat numbers; second set erupts into a marathon Shakedown Street and a series of high‑octane jams that keep the momentum soaring to the finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as moving around onstage during Ramblin' Rose, delivering aggressive, monster guitar tone.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for a muscular, “quite…muscular” bass presence that drives the jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums praised as hypnotic and mid‑’80s style; space segment called “very interesting” and “hypnotic”.
